title: Yacht Charter in Estepona: Costs, Season and What to Know in 2026
html:
Estepona, on the Costa del Sol, offers a refined gateway to the Mediterranean for those seeking a yacht charter. From Puerto Deportivo de Estepona, a typical half-day motorboat hire starts around €250, while a crewed yacht for a day can exceed €1,500. BoatHire24 connects you with verified owners across this sunny coast, making it simple to compare options.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Estepona?

Prices range from €200 for a small motorboat half-day to over €2,000 for a luxury crewed yacht. The exact cost depends on boat size, season, and whether you need a skipper. Use boat rental Estepona listings to filter by budget and trip length. Many owners include fuel and basic insurance in the advertised rate.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Estepona?

In Spain, a recreational licence is required for boats over 10kW engine power if you take the helm yourself. However, most charter companies offer skippered options that require no licence. The operator confirms exact legal requirements at booking, so you can enjoy the coast without paperwork worries.

Where to set off in Estepona

Puerto Deportivo de Estepona is the main marina, with pontoons for vessels up to 20 metres and easy walking access to the old town. Nearby, Cabopino Marina to the east offers a quieter launch point. For day trips, head to the Bay of Estepona or cross the Strait to Gibraltar, roughly 40 nautical miles south. The beaches of Costa Natura and the fishing village of Sabinillas are also popular stop-offs.

Which boat to rent

  1. Small motorboat (5, 6 metres), ideal for couples exploring the coastline, up to 4 guests, from €200 half-day.
  2. RIBs and speedboats, perfect for watersports and quick hops to Marbella, up to 6 guests, from €350 half-day.
  3. Sailing catamaran (12, 15 metres), stable family day out, up to 12 guests, from €700 full-day.
  4. Crewed motor yacht (Azimut or Fairline), luxury with skipper and host, up to 8 guests, from €1,500 full-day.
  5. Large flybridge (Sunseeker or similar), overnight cruises to Gibraltar or Morocco, up to 10 guests, from €2,500 per 24 hours.

For a refined option, see the Fairline listing on BoatHire24, which sleeps five and includes a full crew.

Costs at a glance

  • Price band: €200, €2,500+ per day in EUR
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Trip length: half-day (4 hours) or full-day (8 hours)
  • Capacity: 4, 12 guests depending on boat
  • Currency: EUR

Season and local tips

High season runs from June to September, when temperatures hit 30°C and the sea is calm. The prevailing levante wind can pick up in the afternoon, so morning departures are recommended. A valid licence is not required if you book a skippered charter.

Book your yacht charter in June or September to avoid the July, August crowds; the water is still warm, and morning trips from Puerto Deportivo avoid the levante breeze.

Questions about boat rental in Estepona

Can I rent a boat in Estepona without a licence?

Yes, most charter boats include a skipper, so no licence is required from you.

What is the best boat for a family day in Estepona?

A sailing catamaran offers plenty of deck space and shade for children, and you can anchor in the Bay of Estepona.

How far is Gibraltar by boat from Estepona?

The distance is roughly 40 nautical miles, taking about 1.5 hours at cruising speed on a motor yacht.

Can I book a yacht for a full week in Estepona?

Yes, several multi-day charters are available; the Fairline listing on BoatHire24 offers week-long rates on request.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Estepona or list your own. For 2026, owners are updating their calendars early, so check availability now. If you own a vessel, consider listing it on BoatHire24 to reach thousands of travellers planning their perfect day on the water.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Estepona and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
